content_preview: 2.1. Materials
Poly(vinyl alcohol) (PVA, Mn=74800 g/mol) and ammonium sulfate (AS, AR) were
Journal Pre-proofs

2.2. Preparation of PVA-Agar-AS hydrogel
evidence: ["paragraph_title label with numbering: 2.2. Preparation of PVA-Agar-AS hydrogel"]

content_preview: PVA-Agar-AS hydrogels were synthesized by a combination of FT and soaking methods. Briefly, PVA (1.00 g), different weights (0.10 g, 0.15 g, 0.20 g) of agar and DI water (9.00 g) were put in a vessel.
2.3. Characterization

FT-IR spectra were performed on a Thermo Nicolet Avatar 380 Fourier transform infrared spectrometer by the KBr tablet method in the range of 4000-500 cm⁻¹. The SEM images were recorded on a ZEISS EVO1
The content of water in the hydrogel at different states was calculated according to the formula (1) [49]:
$$ X_{n}=\frac{Q_{endo}}{Q_{f}}\times100\% $$
Where  $ Q_{endo} $ is the melting enthalpy of the sample during the heating process;  $ Q_{f}=333.5 $ J/g is the melting enthalpy of pure water [49].
Journal Pre-proofs
2.4. Mechanical testing

The mechanical performance of the hydrogel was measured on an electronic testing machine (HZ-1003B, Hengzhun Instrument Technology Co., China) at RT. Before tests, the samples were cut into dumbbells.

Oscillatory dynamic mechanical measurements were performed on an Anton Paar MCR 302 rheometer equipped with 25 mm parallel plates and a gap of 1.0 mm. The linear viscoelastic (LVE) response region for
2.5. Calculation of component content

content_preview: A gravimetric method was used to measure the content of each component. The water  $ (W_{H_2O}) $, gel  $ (W_{Gel}) $ and AS contents  $ (W_{AS}) $ in the hydrogels were estimated according to the for
$$ W_{Gel}=\frac{W_{G}\times C_{G}}{W_{w}}\times100\% $$
$$ W_{AS}=\frac{W_{d}-W_{G}\times C_{G}}{W_{w}}\times100\% $$

Where,  $ W_{w} $ and  $ W_{d} $ are the weight of the wet hydrogel and the dried hydrogel respectively. While  $ W_{G} $ is the weight of the wet gel before soaking, and  $ C_{G} $ is the mass fracti

Graphical abstract
Electrical conductivity and Self-healing
Journal Pre-proofs
Highlights
• A method from commercially available sources is reported to obtain functional gel.
• The tensile strength and toughness of the gel are 18.0 MPa and 42.3 MJ/m $ ^{3} $.
• The gel is self-healable and conductive
- This hydrogel might find applications as a biocompatible and bioactive material.
